violations.description: Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. Boxes of food being stored on the walk-in cooler floor.

Cooling of potentially hazardous food. — --Cooked potentially hazardous food was not cooled properly from 140F to 70F or below within two hours. Gravy in the walk-in cooler measured at 103 degrees, employee said it had been removed from heat at approximately 10am. Directed to dispose of product immediately.

General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Nonfood contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in a good repair or sound condition. A pan is being used to hold up 1 side of the freezer in the kitchen. Knob on the cheese dispenser was held together with tape.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored, handled, dispensed in a manner to protect from contamination. Single use food containers placed on top of the equipment in the kitchen have the food contact surface facing up and need to be inverted.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. Bare wood shelves holding food in the walk-in cooler are not easily cleanable and non-absorbent.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Floors underneath the shelving in the dry storage, walk-in freezer, and walk-in cooler need to be cleaned. Floors underneath the kitchen equipment need to be cleaned.
Installed, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
